export_view.value:
Invoicing gives your company tools to manage customers and corporate clients with net terms. Invoicing is available to users on our Advanced and Professional plans.

Once you have created an invoice, you can update the Remittance (payment) details of the invoice to make changes to the linked tickets, void the invoice, or record payments to reconcile the invoice.

Note: A Virtual Cash Terminal (VCT) is not required to record payments on invoices. The transactions recorded here do not appear on the Cash Reconciliation report.

Additional Resources

  • Invoicing Resources

  • Invoicing: Creating and Managing Invoices

  • Invoices: Editing Tickets on Invoices

  • Invoicing: Refunding and Voiding Invoices

Viewing Remittance (Payment) Details

1. On the Navigation Bar, go to Customers → Invoices.

2. On the Invoice Summary screen, you will see all recently created invoices. Click the Options button next to the invoice and select Remittance.

Note: When viewing an invoice, you can also click the Remittance button on the upper-right corner of the page.

3. The Remittance page will show options to apply payment(s), apply discounts to existing tickets, and remove tickets from an invoice.

Applying Payment(s) to an Invoice

Apply a Payment to the Invoice

Under the Apply Payment section of the Remittance page, enter the payment amount the customer is paying and reference notes, and select the payment method. After you have entered the information, click Apply Payment. By default, payments are distributed to the oldest tickets first. If more than one ticket balance is paid, the remainder will carry over to other older tickets.

Under the Invoice Items section, the payment will apply to the tickets. Any ticket that has a balance paid in full will automatically update to Closed status, pending save.

Apply a Payment to a Ticket on the Invoice

If you would like to apply a payment to a specific ticket, you can enter the payment amount under the Payment section of the line item. After entering the payment amount, update the Payment Method and add any notes under the Remittance section of the page.

Payment Date and Revenue Date

Before updating the payment record, users can adjust the payment date and revenue date. If these fields are blank, the payment information will default to the current date when saving remittance.

  • Payment Date - This will update the transaction date in your Cash Flow Summary report. This is NOT the date the revenue/sales are accounted for. If you enter a date before the ticket was advanced to Waiting for Payment status, it will auto-adjust to the same date as the Waiting for Payment status change.

  • Revenue Date - This will adjust the date when revenue/sales are accounted for. If you enter a date before the ticket was advanced to Waiting for Payment status, it will auto-adjust to the same date as the Waiting for Payment status change. This is useful if repair work was completed on one month but was not paid for until the next month, and you want your sales to show the same month the work was completed.

Saving Remittance

Once you have applied the payment(s) and information details, click the Save Remittance button on the right side of the screen.

The invoice will update with a new payment status — Partially Paid if a partial payment was taken, or Paid in Full if the entire invoice has been paid. Any tickets paid in full will update to Closed status automatically.

Note: The tickets paid through an invoice will reflect payments in the Transaction section of the ticket.

Overpayment

When saving remittance, if the invoice has been overpaid, a pop-up will appear, asking how you would like to reconcile the overpayment.

  • Credit Customer - This option will apply the overpayment as a Credit Balance on either the customer's or billing agent's profile, depending on which party was invoiced. The balance can be applied to future tickets/invoices.

  • Ignore Overpayment - This option will not track the overpayment in your records, with the implication that the overpayment was reconciled outside of RepairQ e.g., cutting a check for overpayment back to the customer/billing agent.

START 1.18.X
